name: Orange-Caramel Custard
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 5-cup glass ring mold, combine 1/2 cup sugar and the water. Microwave on HIGH for 6 minutes (stirring after 3 minutes), or until the sugar syrup is just golden; do not overcook. Holding the mold with potholders, slowly rotate the mold until the bottom and sides are coated; set aside to cool.
2. In a 4-cup glass measuring cup, place evaporated milk; microwave on HIGH for 5 minutes. Meanwhile, in a medium bowl, combine eggs, remaining sugar, orange peel, and vanilla; mix well. Gradually stir hot evaporated milk into egg mixture; mix well.
3. Into the prepared mold, pour all but 1 cup of egg mixture. Place the mold back into the microwave. Pour the remaining egg mixture into the mold. Microwave on MEDIUM for 7 to 10 minutes, turning every 3 minutes—custard will be liquid in the center and will set as it cools. Cool on a wire rack; refrigerate at least 6 hours.
4. Run a small knife around the edge of the mold to loosen. Hold the rimmed serving plate over the mold; invert; lift off the mold. Garnish with orange slices and mint, if desired. Makes 10 servings.
